dimensions.note: Combined model + workflow-scope report that makes NO AWS calls and handles no credentials of any kind. The model reads already-stored upstream audit data via the data repository; the report reads it from workflow step executions. There is nothing to vault, scope, or defer to the environment.

Model: info on coalesce completion with accounts/instance/role counts via structured placeholders. Report: info on run start and on finish (accounts + degraded flag), plus an info collection summary and a warn on the degraded path — all routed through tryLog, which tolerates a missing/throwing logger so logging never affects correctness. Structured placeholders throughout; no secrets handled or logged.

Model: coalesce throws a single descriptive 'run the audits first' error only when BOTH upstreams produced zero usable rows (covered by test); every throw precedes the writeResource loop, so no partial state. Per-artifact undecodable bytes and schema-mismatched rows are tolerantly skipped and counted (parseAll bad / readSpec skip), never thrown. Report: never throws — the entire collect+coalesce+render is wrapped so any unexpected failure yields a degraded:true, structurally-valid report with a stub markdown (covered by the exploding-context test).

tests/ holds the shared-core unit suite (coverage_test.ts) plus the model read+write/smoke suite and the report collect+render suite. Model: readSpec latest-version-per-name selection and deleted/null-byte skipping, parseAll tolerant reject counting, the both-upstreams-empty throw, IAM-only matrix, and the full write path (one coverage-<accountId> per account + exactly one summary, skipped count threaded into sources.skipped, dataHandles == writes, no AWS client exposed). Report: modelType filtering + spec routing + skip counting, renderMarkdown section presence, the markdown+json-only / no-csv contract, and the never-throws degraded path. Colocated tests pin BOTH entrypoints (model and report) to the manifest, the two model specs + single coalesce method, and the report name/scope contract. 37 tests pass / 0 fail after the schema tightening; no test relied on passthrough into a written row.

Pure read-and-derive over already-stored upstream data, no external mutation. Model coverage rows are keyed coverage-<accountId> and the summary is a fixed 'summary' key, so re-runs overwrite the same instances deterministically (rows and roleNames are sorted for stable output). Resilient to partial/corrupt upstream artifacts (skipped + counted) and to a degraded upstream sweep. The report adds a degraded:true outer guard. No created resources to orphan.

Makes NO external HTTP/AWS calls. Its only contracts are the upstream resource schemas it consumes (the StackSet instance/summary and IAM role/scan_error rows), which it re-validates with zod safeParse per row before use — the model via dataRepository.findAllForModel/getContent, the report via the workflow's stepExecutions data handles.

Creates no SDK clients, network connections, file handles, or temp files. Reads stored bytes (TextDecoder over getContent results) and returns model writes / markdown + JSON; there is nothing to clean up or track on any code path.

README, manifest, and test fixtures use clearly-fictional placeholders (ACCT_ALPHA / ACCT_BETA account labels, acme-ss stackset, generic role names); zero 12-digit account-id literals appear anywhere in the package, and no real IPs, hostnames, or topology. Vendor names were scrubbed to generic placeholders. The data-shape comments referencing '12-digit account id' describe the field semantics, not real values.

The model's OWN written, CEL-queryable resource schemas are now plain z.object with no .passthrough(): CoverageRowSchema (_lib/coverage.ts) backs the per-account 'coverage' resource and SummaryResourceSchema (aws_integration_coverage.ts) backs the singleton 'summary' resource — both verified field-for-field against the objects passed to writeResource('coverage', ...) / writeResource('summary', ...) and matching 1:1 (CoverageRow: 13 fields; summary: 17 fields incl. the nested sources object's exactly 5 keys), so z.object strips no real field and CEL expression validation now sees the exact resource shape. The MIRRORED UPSTREAM input schemas in _lib (InstanceSchema, the upstream SummarySchema, RoleSchema, IamErrorSchema) intentionally retain .passthrough() + safeParse: these are tolerant DECODERS of another producer's output, NOT this model's own resources, so passthrough there is correct by design and is deliberately left in place.

Both model specs (coverage, summary) declare lifetime: 'infinite' and garbageCollection: 10. The report persists nothing of its own (the host stores report-<name> / report-<name>-json), so GC config is not applicable to the report half.

Read-only coalesce model, not a CRUD lifecycle. coalesce re-derives the per-account coverage matrix + rollup summary each run from upstream data; there is no resource this model owns to create/update/delete, and re-running coalesce is the refresh mechanism, so a separate sync method is unnecessary.
coalesce makes no AWS calls and performs no mutation — it only reads stored data and writes derived swamp rows. There is no destructive action a policy/live pre-flight label would gate.

Mechanical check: the coverage spec is keyed coverage-<accountId> (unique per account) and the summary spec is the singleton key 'summary'. Distinct specs, distinct key prefixes, single writer (coalesce) per spec — no cross-spec or cross-method collision possible. Schema-write conformance: coverage rows are CoverageRow objects matching CoverageRowSchema 1:1; the summary object's 17 fields match SummaryResourceSchema 1:1 with no placeholders (each is computed from rollup/result or echoed from the source ids).

The model legitimately reads OTHER models' stored data via dataRepository.findAllForModel + getContent (a cross-model coalesce of the stackset-audit and iam-role-audit instances). The readResource-vs-dataRepository guidance concerns reading back a model's OWN state in update/delete/sync — this model never reads back its own coverage/summary rows; it reads upstream producers' data, for which dataRepository is the correct API. readSpec keeps the latest active version per data name and skips deleted/undecodable artifacts. Not an issue.

Initial publish (2026.06.13.0). model.upgrades carries a single identity entry whose toVersion equals model.version; the colocated test asserts that invariant. upgradeAttributes is identity (old => old), correct for an initial publish with no prior stored instances to migrate. (The report is unversioned by the model upgrade mechanism; its contract is pinned by name/scope in the colocated tests.)
